findings.finding: The validation gate described in Spec Requirement 7 and Verification Strategy item 4 has no durable enforcement mechanism beyond a one-shot review of this lifecycle's implementation commit. No task adds a precommit hook, CI check, runtime assertion, or extends read_synthesizer_gate to cross-reference events.log for the two required validation events. After this lifecycle ships, any future commit can flip synthesizer_overnight_enabled: true with a single-line YAML edit and never record the validation events.
findings.evidence_quote: confirm the runtime gate stays `false` after this lifecycle's implementation lands — no flag-flip in the same commit. The flag is flipped only after the operator records `synthesizer_validated_interactive` and `synthesizer_validated_overnight` events in `lifecycle/{feature}/events.log` (Requirement 7) in a follow-up commit.

findings.finding: T8 probe (c) skipif-on-missing-corpus silently degrades the calibration suite from 3 probes to 2 when the cited lifecycle directory is archived/renamed, while spec acceptance ('3+ test cases passing') still trips green via pytest exit 0. Spec-acceptance semantic and long-term calibration coverage are misaligned with the project's lifecycle-mutability convention.
findings.evidence_quote: Mark probe (c) `pytest.mark.skipif(not path.exists(), ...)` to allow the test to skip cleanly if the corpus changes.

findings.finding: T7's helper-extraction is reduced to a soft contract: T7's verification grep accepts inline-only implementations, and Veto Surface authorizes dropping the helper signature without specifying the integration-only test shape, fixture, or entry-point name. T10 Tests 1 and 2 import a helper that may not exist under the veto branch.
findings.evidence_quote: if inline-only is preferred (no helper extraction), Task 7's helper signature is dropped and Task 10's first two test cases test via integration only.
